claim: Thermocyclic tests used specimens with φ 5 mm working part, 80% thermal expansion restriction, heating by contact electric resistance up to 873 K, and cooling by compressed air to 313 K, resulting in cyclic alternation 873–313 K (Fig. 1a).
evidence_text: For thermocyclic tests, samples with a f 5 mm working part were used in a chamber (Fig. 1a) with a degree of 80% thermal expansion restriction. Heating of samples by electric resistance up to 873 K was made by a contact method in limited condition, thus ensuring cyclic thermoplastic deformation. The cyclic alternation of temperature from 873 to 313 K was promoted by cooling to 313 K by a jet of compressed air.

claim: TEM thin foils were prepared by electropolishing in a solution of chromic acid (60 g CrO3) and phosphoric acid (400 mL H3PO4) at 20–50 °C, 15–20 V, and 5–8 A/cm².
evidence_text: Thin foils were prepared by electropolishing in a solution of chromic (60 g CrO3) and phosphoric (400 mL $\mathrm { H } _ { 3 } \mathrm { P O } _ { 4 }$) acid solution at 20 to 50 °C. The electropolishing was conducted at 15 to 20 V and 5 to 8 A/cm2.

claim: In rotating bending fatigue tests at 293 K, the stress amplitude was 420 MPa at 0.1×10^6 cycles and decreased to 240 MPa at 50×10^6 cycles; at 673 K, from 500 MPa to 380 MPa; under thermocycling 293 K ↔ 673 K, from 480 MPa to 360 MPa.

The great strengthening capacity of the Fe-Mn-Al-C alloy is related to mechanical twinning as the basic mechanism of plastic deformation in its austenitic structure, and deformation twins act as obstacles to further plastic deformation by sliding and twinning.
evidence_text: The great capacity of strengthening revealed by the Fe-Mn-Al-C alloy is related to features of its austenitic structure during plastic deformation (Fig. 5), whose basic mechanism is mechanical twinning. Deformation twins act as obstacles to further plastic deformation by sliding and twinning.

claim: The strain hardening curve of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenite at a strain rate of 1 mm/min shows true stress increasing from 500 MPa at zero strain to 1400 MPa at 0.4 true strain.
evidence_text: Fig. 5. Strain hardening of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenite. Strain rate 1 mm/min True Strain True Stress (MPa) 0.0 500 0.1 800 0.2 1000 0.3 1200 0.4 1400

claim: In the force-deflection diagram for elasto-plastic bending of a 50 mm diameter specimen, the first cycle recorded a deflection of 150 mm at a load of 30×10³ N, the second cycle 120 mm at 40×10³ N, and the twelfth cycle 100 mm at 35×10³ N.
evidence_text: Fig. 4. Force-deflection diagram. Variations with increasing number of cycles. Specimen f 50 mm. (Q) applied load and (H) deflection 1st Cycle 150 30 2nd Cycle 120 40 12nd Cycle 100 35
